metadata.title: Wait Youre Using Ai To Invest In

metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The idea is building an AI system to help you track all of your investing activity in one place. The honest version deliberately does not claim to have cracked the code of markets. It avoids promising 40% annual returns from some proprietary algorithm. Its credibility comes from restraint — tracking and clarity, not hype-driven performance guarantees.

artifactLines: The idea is building an AI system to help you track all of your investing activity in one place.
The honest version deliberately does not claim to have cracked the code of markets.
It avoids promising 40% annual returns from some proprietary algorithm.
Its credibility comes from restraint — tracking and clarity, not hype-driven performance guarantees.

beats.narration_text: The part that reassures me is that this system tracks everything without promising 40% returns from a proprietary algorithm. I want to build a modest version for my own portfolio. Help me (1) list exactly what 'all of it' should mean to track — holdings, theses, and the reason I bought each, (2) design the honest guardrail language so it never drifts into cracked-the-code promises, and (3) set up one weekly check where the system flags where my original reasoning no longer holds.
